frameworks.name: high-gamma cortical decoding
frameworks.one_line: Power-spectrogram decoding of ECoG to read out face information from cortex.

frameworks.name: Haxby invariant/changeable face-processing model
frameworks.one_line: Dominant model assigning invariant face features to ventral and changeable to lateral temporal cortex.

core_question: Are invariant and changeable aspects of faces represented in anatomically distinct ventral vs lateral temporal cortex, as the dominant model claims?
key_assumptions: Spectral power modulations index local neural population activity relevant to face processing
Decoding accuracy from ECoG reflects information content of underlying cortex
Static and dynamic facial expressions tap invariant and changeable face features respectively
manipulations: Stimulus type: static vs dynamic facial expressions
Cortical site: ventral vs lateral temporal cortex
key_findings: Ventral temporal cortex decoded both invariant and changeable face aspects better than lateral temporal cortex
Face-vs-pattern discrimination relied on 50–150 Hz power; fear-vs-happy used 60–150 Hz and <30 Hz
Task-relevant attention boosted decoding by >10% across frequencies in ventral but not lateral cortex
Searchlight peaks were near middle fusiform gyrus, with right-hemisphere superiority
interpretations: Results challenge the dominant invariant/changeable anatomical dissociation between ventral and lateral temporal cortex
Ventral temporal cortex carries integrated face information modulated by attention
